select_when: The next level is reachable but not yet automatic, so the team needs stretch that grows capability without overwhelming it.
Instruction or support must match current capability rather than an idealized end state.
Progress depends on sequencing, calibration, and feedback rather than on heroic leaps.
Novel work requires iterative progress and well-shaped structure to push the boundary of current competence.
avoid_when: The challenge is so mismatched to readiness that it either produces no growth or creates confusion, dependence, or withdrawal.
People are jumping into analysis before getting clear on the output, diagnosis, and current capability boundary.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on stretch-fit calibration, sequencing, and readiness-aware support rather than on broad developmental theory.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor educational-stage sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on calibration, feedback loops, and structured stretch.
open_questions: The source reaches into unknown-unknown problem solving, but the operational file stays anchored to calibrated next-step design.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says the model is strongest when the next level is reachable but not automatic, when support must match current capability, and when sequencing plus feedback drive growth.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns about mismatched challenge and about premature analysis before defining the real developmental output.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about readiness and support fit; the typed input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model creates calibrated stretch and staged progression; the short output label compresses that operational move.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses readiness inside a structured development system, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
